topic.what: Benchmark dataset of 15 real-world Python CVEs where vulnerabilities were introduced across multiple commits, each individually benign to per-commit SAST
topic.how: Manually annotated multi-commit introduction chains with per-commit and cumulative SAST evaluations using Semgrep and Bandit
topic.why_matters: Reveals systematic blind spot in snapshot-based SAST tools; 87% of cross-commit chains invisible to per-commit analysis, 73% invisible even cumulatively

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of CVEs detected when analyzing each contributing commit in isolation
characteristics.context: CCDR metric; both detections are qualitatively poor (suppressed alerts, minor CWE only)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of CVEs detected when analyzing full pre-fix codebase with all commits present
characteristics.evidence: 4/15 CVEs caught when full codebase analyzed
characteristics.context: CDR metric; 73% remain invisible even with complete snapshot

characteristics.what_itasures: Time between first and last contributing commit in vulnerability chain
characteristics.context: Vulnerability chains can emerge over weeks to years

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average number of distinct commits introducing each vulnerability
characteristics.context: Multi-commit pattern requires ≥2 commits per CVE

characteristics.what_it_measures: CWE pattern types detectable by snapshot-based SAST
characteristics.evidence: Only CWE-94 and CWE-321 caught; custom wrappers and absent guards invisible
characteristics.context: Custom wrapper opacity and absent-guard patterns evade all SAST rules
